purpose: First research demo for checking that CLI output, graph artifacts, and the localhost web dashboard expose the same backend-owned research state.

topic.title: ISO 26262 safety case drift
topic.objective: Track whether ASIL decomposition evidence still supports the current safety case after a supplier tool qualification change.

run.input: counterargument: supplier tool confidence may be stale after the latest qualification delta

commands: crb topic create "ISO 26262 safety case drift" --objective "Track whether ASIL decomposition evidence still supports the current safety case after a supplier tool qualification change." --json
crb run start topic_iso_26262_safety_case_drift --input "counterargument: supplier tool confidence may be stale after the latest qualification delta" --json
crb graph export topic_iso_26262_safety_case_drift --scope latest --format json --output iso26262-graph.json --json
crb graph view topic_iso_26262_safety_case_drift --scope latest --format html --output iso26262-graph.html --json
web_checks: Confirm Overview shows the current best hypothesis initialized from the objective.
Confirm Runs shows run_5e3826f4ce35 as a queue_request before a worker claims it.
Confirm Queue shows queue_5e3826f4ce35 and the same run id.
Confirm Memory and Graph retain the authority notice that projections are not a source of truth.
failure_modes_attacked: README command drift from actual CLI commands
CLI and web dashboard exposing different run or queue identifiers
web dashboard hiding that a run is queued but not yet claimed
authority notice or conflict visibility disappearing from first-user docs
